assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
The contemplated hedging relationship would use a hedging instrument with a party external to the reporting entity.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets or liabilities as eligible hedged items, which maps to the fair value hedge model for a recognised foreign-currency-denominated receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions, including qualifying highly probable intragroup foreign-currency transactions, as a distinct hedge-accounting model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge-accounting model for foreign exchange risk.
